# orcasecurity_integration_monday_resource (Resource)

Manages a **Monday.com credentials resource** in Orca Security.

Orca splits the Monday.com integration into two pieces:

1. The **resource** (this resource) — holds the Monday API token. Orca validates the
   token, derives the account slug from it, and stores the token in its secret store.
2. The **template** (managed by
   [`orcasecurity_integration_monday_template`](integration_monday_template.md)) — holds
   the board / group / field-mapping settings. One credential can back many templates.

Under the hood this creates an entry under `POST /api/external_service/resources`
with `service_name = "monday"`. Updates and deletes go to
`/api/external_service/resources/<id>`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Monday.com credentials resource, created from a Monday API token. Orca derives the
# account slug from the token and stores the token in its secret store. Use the returned
# id as resource_id on orcasecurity_integration_monday_template.
resource "orcasecurity_integration_monday_resource" "demo" {
  name      = "monday-prod"
  api_token = var.monday_api_token
}

output "monday_resource_id" {
  value = orcasecurity_integration_monday_resource.demo.id
}
```

## Argument Reference

* `name` — (Required, String) Human-friendly name for the Monday.com resource.
* `api_token` — (Required, String, Sensitive) Monday.com API token. Stored in Orca's
  secret store and never returned by the API.

## Attribute Reference

* `id` — (String) Orca resource identifier (UUID). Feed this into the `resource_id`
  argument on `orcasecurity_integration_monday_template`.
* `account_slug` — (String) Monday.com account slug Orca derives from the API token.

## Import

```bash
terraform import orcasecurity_integration_monday_resource.demo <resource-uuid>
```
